Name: Carson
Review Title:
1.0 out of 5 stars
 CAUSED NONSTOP CYSTIC ACNE BREAKOUT WITHIN DAYS, DO NOT BUY
Review Content:
I got this cleanser to help my skin retain moisture because my lifelong oily skin has become dry combo skin over the past couple years, and also wanted to reduce sebum filaments and blackheads. I was obsessed with this cleanser from the first wash that I used it, and I used it religiously for 3 weeks morning and night along with their ANUA exfoliating toner and their rice ceramide skim barrier serum, with my normal moisturizer.
On day 3 I developed a cyst on my cheek, this has happened few times in my life so I brushed it off and just figured my skin was purging — a good thing to me!
Day 5, two more cysts appeared in other areas of my face. That was weird to me, but again assumed skin was purging. Luckily I have an incredible retinol-AHA/BHA combo routine, so that would fairly quickly diminish the cysts and the red marks that they’d leave.
By day 8 those initial three cysts were mostly gone… only to wake up on day 9 with THREE NEW CYSTS, all in different places… I was so upset and could not understand what was happening! My skin just doesn’t do that, it never has!
On top of the cystic acne I was finding more and more large bumpy whiteheads scattered around my face, which is also abnormal for my skin. That’s when I got a suspicion it was the cleanser, but I was in denial. I loved it. I was using it exactly correct too and everything.
At the end of day 14 I got a cyst on the inner end of my eyebrow, and by the afternoon of day 15 I had ANOTHER one in the middle of the same eyebrow!!! I was crying in frustration at this point because these cysts were making my face SWELL! Especially ones that appeared along my jaw or on my cheek. I had wore makeup only twice within this two week period so I was even more confused as to what could’ve been getting trapped underneath to cause all this.
Day 19 I noticed I was JUST beginning to develop a cyst on my cheek so I cut off the cleanser right then and there. Went back to my SA cleanser while I look for a different brand oil cleanser and my skin is back to normal now. That last cyst never fully developed after I stopped btw.
I have never had an issue with any type of cleanser, or even any product, before and certainly never anything like this. My skin would have definitely been scarred in every area of my face (both cheeks, chin, both sides of jaw, just below forehead) with the frequency, size and rate of this cystic breakout, had it not been for my combo routine I mentioned. Unbelievable!!!
I should note that I did not suspend using their exfoliating toner and rice ceramide serum, those have been AMAZING!
Get a different oil cleanser, do not buy!!!

Name: Chris
Review Title:
1.0 out of 5 stars
 This should be illegal!
Review Content:
My face is still healing from the pimples they gave me, used to have a whole colony on my chin, breaking out in places I never even broke out before. Even with the help of salicylic acid in my skincare routine I keep breaking out on my chin and soon enough my cheeks. I thought it was purging but it was nonstop and painful! I keep getting them on top of each other like fungal and hormonal cysts. I broke out more and more. Now I have to admit it was this cleanser. Definitely something in it that is acne and inflammation prone. The positive reviews are because they haven't used it long enough to notice fungal acne! Gave it a month before I realized it wasn't 'purging'.
Update: I updated the 3rd picture in pink shirt which is as of today. I am left with acne scars and some breakouts here and there after 2 weeks of discontinued use, but not a whole colony of them like when I was using the anua cleansing oil after a couple weeks. Those tiny fungal acne bumps become inflamed into cysts, one by one. I have some indented acne scars, my skin was fine, never broke out like this before, i know some reviews must be bought/fake that is why there is so much marketing going on. I am using tea tree oil, cetella Tricica, Obagi and Paula's choice SA occasionally, along with high end skin barrier repair creams from Amore Pacific and Shiseido. My skin IS improving but it also takes a lot to get to this point (little leftover bumps are still breaking out into big pimples). I dont know how to see my inlaws this upcoming Christmas week. I wanted to repurchased DHC cleansing oil for double cleansing but then I saw this was bestseller, big mistake! I just wanted to improve my skin a bit for the holiday season, seeing the family and in-laws. This has costed me time, stress, money and mental health. I would sue them if I knew how. Worst thing I know this will continue to sell and they will never read this.
Update: I just wanted to update that I did a tca 30 peel with numbing cream to cure all of this. (Very) Painful, but last resort. It was definitely this cleanser. Hope it helps.

Name: klew
Review Title:
1.0 out of 5 stars
 BAD BREAKOUTS
Review Content:
If i could leave zero stars i would. for starts i have dry/sensitive skin. i was looking into starting to use an oil cleanser because most cleansers leave my face feeling dry and tight so i thought an oil cleanser would be a better alternative. i saw so many people on tiktok raving about this product so of course i purchased (easily influenced- WONT be anymore)
i am not someone who gets breakouts often. in all my 26 years i have rarely ever experienced acne/breakouts. i used this for two weeks and each morning would wake up with a new pimple. a lot of them would be those painful under the skin ones. idk why i kept using it, i guess i just saw so many things about it i figured there was no way it could be this product even though nothing new was added to my routine besides this.
after a quick google search and also reading people’s experiences with this cleanser on reddit and also noninfluencers tiktokers experiences as well i quickly realized it was in fact THIS product.
i havent used in for two weeks now and my skin is still in shambles. this completely ruined my skin.
also if you’ve seen people saying this removed sebaceous filaments, it DOES NOT. i would massage this into my nose and chin for multiple minutes and nothing. if anything the things you might see on your finger tips is balled up dry skin. a oil cleanser will not remove your sebaceous filaments.
looking back and searching this product on tiktok it shows majority of the videos are paid sponsorships/ADs. idk why it doesn’t show that when you see it on your fyp.
i will never buy from this brand again alone because with my research it seems to cause issues for a lot of peoples skin.
moral of the story don’t use this. don’t buy this. also don’t trust anything influencers recommend. everything i’ve ever bought that was recommended from a tiktok influencer sucked.

Name: Corinna Garmon
Review Title:
1.0 out of 5 stars
 DON'T DO IT! Worked great but now give me zits
Review Content:
I was going to leave a glowing review because it really does clean out your pores. It removed lots of gunk including some blackheads. The first time I used it all the little bits of gunk that came out felt like sandpaper on my hands. At first I thought my skin was purging but the zits around my mouth area kept coming. I stopped using the product for a few days and they cleared up. Then I felt little bumps all under my skin like clogged pores so I decided to use the cleansing oil again. It cleaned them out but guess what! Now I have 5 painful zits around my mouth area again. I guess it's not for everyone. After I clear up I may try using it everywhere except around my mouth and see what happens.
Update: After the painful acne around my mouth then I started developing large cystic acne around my left temple into the hairline. I immediately threw the product in the trash. After reading reviews about people developing bacterial and fungal acne from this product I treated with mupricin 2 nights ago. I also changed my pillow cases as I side sleep on my left. I woke up to the acne looking better. I bought benzol peroxide and retinol acne treatments. Treated yesterday and last night and woke up to much better skin.
What a huge mistake in using this product.
Update: Into week 2 of not using the cleansing oil and still developing cystic acne that I'm having to treat. Developed three small ones in my hairline in the past 2 days.
Update: Week 3 - cystic acne has calmed down but my face is still a mess. Scarring from the cystic acne. The small hard bumps are coming to the surface.
Update: Still breaking out. I'm going to have to wear so much concealer for Thanksgiving dinner.
December 11, 2024: I am still breaking out in painful cysts around my mouth and hairline. I have to use OTC acne treatment to keep them under control. Using this product was a horrible decision!

Name: Gracie Marie
Review Title:
1.0 out of 5 stars
 DO NOT BUY PRODUCT UNLESS YOU WANT YOUR CONFIDENCE DEPLETED.
Review Content:
Okay, I want to start off with I used this product one time. Just once. I also have combination skin but even then I NEVER used to have any acne like this. I found this product because I was having some issues with my pores and some blackheads, and I bought it to get rid of sebaceous filaments. A lot of the reviews had 5 stars , and I mean a lot. I used it just like it said on the packaging. Rubbed on my nose and cheeks for 5 minutes, diluted it with water, and even used a water based cleanser after. It did what it was supposed to do at first (I thought.) I was happy with the results after using. BUT however after about 5 days to a week later my skin started getting suuuper itchy and dry. I started getting these small raised bumps on my cheeks, and then spread to my chin. I learned that I developed fungal acne, and now what I think has turned into fungal AND bacterial acne. I’ve had clear skin for pretty much my entire life aside from hormonal pimples or just random pimples popping up. I now have what I think are CYSTS developing under my skin (WHAT). This has been so painful to go through mentally, emotionally and physically. It crushes me as a woman who was happy and confident to have clear skin, to wash my face and feel all of the pimples and small bumps. With how bad it’s gotten, It leaves me in tears to look at myself. I’ve had this on my face for 3-4 months now, I don’t know how to get rid of it yet. All I know is please for the love of God save your skin, your confidence or just overall your sanity. DONT buy this crap.
Love yourselves
-M

Name: Mila E.
Review Title:
1.0 out of 5 stars
 This DESTROYED my skin
Review Content:
I really wanted to like this product. I did research before buying it and all the steps involved to ensure I used it correctly; because the reason why a lot of people have issues with a product like this is because of incorrect usage. To preface, I have been on accutane once and am currently experiencing hormonal acne. I have been back and forth with my dermatologist on working on my hormonal acne but me purchasing this product was NOT to help with that; but rather with cleansing and ensuring all makeup and pore clogging impurities were out of my skin.

I have a relatively basic skincare routine and still deal with scarring from my time on accutane as well as the hormonal acne. Before purchasing this product, I was just using the ELF holy hydration makeup remover (love it and it works wonders). I used the ANUA oil cleanser TWICE and my skin and skin barrier has entirely destroyed. My skin is now drier and more textured than ever. I have little whitehead like bumps all over my face, including my eyelids. Where I was experiencing these breakouts is places I have never had before. I feel more self-conscious about my acne now and my experience with this product is just so bad. It has been about 5 days since I stopped using this and my skin has still not gone back to normal. I have simplified my routine down to hydrating basics and I am hoping it gets resolved soon.

Before buying this product, please do your research. Just because it is trending on TikTok or Instagram, doesn't mean it is good. I am really self-concious about my skin right now and I hope no one has to go through the same thing I am going through right now. I am returning the product for my money back and am hoping my skin goes back to normal soon. Please DO NOT BUY.

Name: M. Yee
Review Title:
1.0 out of 5 stars
 Adverse effect
Review Content: I’ve been using this for 1.5 months. I’m in my late 30’s & do not have sensitive skin. People say there’s a strong scent but my bottle, the scent was very light. Almost unnoticeable and my olfactory is quite sensitive. So that’s strange. My nighttime routine is double cleansing and I’ve used oil cleansers from various brands with no skin issues. I heard about this from a mom I follow on IG and heard rave reviews from a K Beauty YouTuber who stated it’s an immensely popular item. 2wks into using it, I noticed texture on my chin like clogged pores. Didn’t think anything of it. 3rd wk I felt more bumps on the side of my face and when I used the product, I did feel stuff in my skin which meant it was getting rid of something off my skin or from my pores, just none of the black heads some people claim. Since the writing is all in Korean, there’s really no clear direction to follow if you can’t read it. I decided to just look online to see if there’s some step I’m not doing that’s required by this brand. Lo and below, you need to emulsify it after the 1min of using it, prior to washing it off. I started doing that but I ended up with more clogged pores throughout my face and my temples started to break out really bad. This is the only new product I added to my skincare routine so it wasn’t hard to pinpoint the cause. I stopped using it 3 days ago and my skin is already healing. Very unfortunate b/c I was really enjoying the product

Name: Vchavez
Review Title:
1.0 out of 5 stars
 Fungal. Acne.
Review Content: I truly love the herbal smell of this oil cleanser and it relaxed me every time I would use it and it really took away my makeup fairly well except for the mascara which I would have to use a cotton pad and miscellar water for. It made my skin look so clean and refreshed. But then maybe a week in that’s when I noticed my face would get these little red bumps. I just thought maybe it was a little irritating or the water was too hot when I would cleanse. Mind you, I always followed this oil cleanser with the water based cleanser they recommend afterwards. But by the second week, the red bumps were bigger and more apparent on my lower jawline, cheeks and chin. Then they started appearing on my forehead. They wouldn’t go away and more just kept appearing. After stopping the Anya cleansers, they finally calmed down, but now I’ve been dealing with this fungal acne that started spreading to my neck even! It’s slowly getting better, but it’s been about 2 months now and the picture is what it looks like still currently. I also used their soothing ampoule but it just made it worse as well. Skin got better after I stopped using Anua all together and just went back to my old routine. Definitely would not recommend if you’re a really oily and sensitive girly.

Name: Blue eyes gone wrong
Review Title:
1.0 out of 5 stars
 This destroyed my skin
Review Content:
So I’ll admit - I should have been more careful. I read the reviews and thought this stuff would be amazing. I made sure I knew how to use it properly and have a good water based cleanser to follow. I double triple and quadruple checked the oils that are used to make sure they play nice with my skin. And it seemed like a good fit…except I didn’t look at the other ingredients. The primary (first) ingredient isn’t a cleansing oil, it’s Ethylhexyl Palmitate and hoo boy if you have acne prone skin you should avoid this ingredient like the plague.

For the first week or so, it made my skin glow, red spots were fading fast, and I was sold on this product. I was still getting acne but hoping this would clear it up. But it didn’t. It got worse, fast. I did more research and realized the error of my ways. I’m now trying to clear up one of the worst and most painful breakouts I’ve ever had. We’re talking swollen, deep, cystic type acne as well as the more run of the mill closed comedones. It’s awful and it hurts so bad. I should have checked every ingredient.

If you don’t have acne prone skin however I can see why people would think it’s awesome.

Review Content: (i have sensitive skin so this may very for everyone. !!!IF YOU DO HAVE SENSITIVE SKIN!!! i would recommend consulting a dermatologist before jumping into a new skin product.) i got this because i have A LOT of sebaceous filaments on my nose, cheek area, and chin, and some on my forehead, apart from that my skin was finally starting to clear up. i had seen plenty of videos saying that using an oil cleanser would help remove them. the first few days my skin was looking very good and glowy, i noticed some filaments noticeably going away, but oh was this just the beginning. after the first few days my cheek and forehead area started to break out a bit. noting i do double cleansing how its “supposed” to be done. (apply the oil cleanser to dry skin always, massage in areas with fingers in circular motions one way for a couple minutes, then the other way, next emulsify with water, and last use your cleanser [foaming] of choice.) even with doing how its “supposed” to be done i started breaking out, and i made the horrible decision to just keep using it, and blamed my break out on my period. now that i kept using it and finally came to the conclusion it was NOT my period breakouts that i normally get. i currently have a horrible break out of my forehead and chin and a breakout on my cheek area and nose. once again, PLEASE if you have sensitive skin go to a dermatologist and don’t just jump into something new and make the same mistake i did.

If you're struggling with acne though or other skin issues (i.e dryness, redness), I do highly encourage you to Google "skin barrier damage" to see what exactly is going on and what your skin needs to help it heal. I did that at the suggestion of another reviewer and lo and behold...what your skin barrier actually needs to heal and remain healthy isn't even found in this product.

Review Title:

 Not for me
Review Content: I really wanted this product to work so bad. I used this for about just under two weeks. I would only use it at night time and I would rub around my nose and on my chin for anywhere from 3 to 7 minutes. It definitely did pull out some sebum and Would exfoliate my skin. The constant exfoliating of my skin led to really dry and itchy, irritated skin specifically on my chin. I started to get whiteheads, which I have never experienced before at the age of 31. Then I started to get cystic, acne, and texture all over my chin. Everywhere else it seems that my pores were even more clogged than before. I have stopped using this product and went back to the basics like cerave face cleanser and a very light day and night lotion. Pimple patches have helped a bit. I have never experienced acne like this in my life and I am kicking myself for falling victim to trendy skincare products on social media. I have combination skin and never have any breakouts. I really just wanted this product to remove the clogged pores on my nose and chin. I cannot believe how much damage this has caused, and I feel like it’s going to take a long time to heal, specifically for the cystic acne bumps. I would say, if you have fair combination skin, and rarely any pimples or breakouts, to just keep it simple and stick to the basics.

I sincerely wish I could rate this product as a 0. I’m sure like many of you saw this product on TikTok or Instagram and were interested because you struggle with sebaceous filaments or large pores. I really wanted to love this product but the damage this has done to my skin will take MONTHS to fix.

I used this product for almost two months when I started to notice comedones on the areas where I had targeted with the oil and I thought it was “purging” but it wasn’t. “Purging” is not a real concept and if you are breaking out after using a product it’s because your skin is unhappy and doesn’t like the product.

Once I stopped using the product, the problem got worse. After a visit to the dermatologist and two visits to an esthetician, I am now on oral antibiotics because I have huge pus related infections on my face. They are not normal pimples. I pop them and I have more the next day in different areas of my face. I have never, even when I was a hormonal teenager, struggled with acne like I am now.
Date: Reviewed in the United States on May 19, 2024
text_ko:

It took me months to clear up my skin and my skin was so good. And then a bit after I started this product, I began breaking out horrifically. I never suspected it because it was always removing my sebum but then I also had no new lifestyle/diet/stress changes so I didn’t know what else it could’ve been.

A whole month has passed since I began using it and I’m breaking out in these tiny little red bumps in places I haven’t broken out in since middle school. Forehead bumps, chin bumps, cheek bumps. Everywhere I focused the cleanser on, it broke out more. Me, not knowing this, kept focusing on those areas and they became exponentially worse.

I only figured out after seeing a bunch of people all over skincare Reddit and TikTok discussing it. I really wish I found out sooner. This damage to my skin barrier is going to take months to repair.

Review Title:

 Ruined my skin
Review Content:
I recently decided to try out a skincare line that had been heavily advertised. Excited to see the promised results, I eagerly incorporated the products into my daily routine. However, much to my dismay, instead of the radiant complexion I had hoped for, my skin began to develop blackheads and a plethora of pimples.

The disappointment was palpable as I realized that I had unwittingly become a casualty of clever marketing tactics. It's disheartening to think that the very products I had trusted to improve my skin had ended up causing more harm than good.

In hindsight, I realize the importance of being discerning when it comes to skincare products. Blindly falling for flashy advertisements can lead to regrettable outcomes, as I've unfortunately experienced firsthand.

To anyone considering purchasing this particular skincare line, I urge you to proceed with caution. Take the time to thoroughly research the products and consider seeking advice from skincare professionals before making any decisions. Your skin's health is too precious to risk on a whim.

But then it broke me out like no body's business. I was getting huge pimples and multiple closed comodones on my chin when I never had them before. I tried researching if there would be a purge stage with it, and all the results said oil cleansing doesn't cause purging. It might have been too much for my skin; ingredients, scent, etc. I was under the impression that this didn't have a scent, but it definitely does. I did LOVE the way it felt after I used it, and it did reduce the the sebum on my nose, so I guess that's a plus! I ended up returning it, but I still wish that it worked for me.

Name: Emily Webster
Review Title:
2.0 out of 5 stars
 Not Worth the Breakouts
Review Content:
When I first used the product, my skin was relevantly clear aside one or two prominent zits and several blackheads. I had gotten it after buying into others' reviews, saying it helped remove facial filament and blackheads from their pores. After using it in my problems areas (chin, cheeks, nose) I did not notice any immediate difference is the existing acne, and only noting that it removed dried skin cells per most face washes.

The next day, I had broken out much more than the day before. I had assumed that it just happened because I was trying a new product with oil, and it might take some time getting used to it. I continued to use it for over a week, with the foam cleanser, and my skin just kept getting worse.

As a makeup remover, it works great. It really melts the makeup after wearing it for several hours. But it is not worth the breakout when I have a makeup remover that works just as well, but doesn't make me breakout as badly.

I do not recommend this be added to your daily skincare routine, even if you wear make every single day and only use it as a makeup remover.

Name: Philly
Review Title:
2.0 out of 5 stars
 Great Scent, But May Not Suit Acne-Prone Skin
Review Content:
The Anua Heartleaf Pore Control Cleansing Oil has a lovely, refreshing scent and a lightweight texture that makes it enjoyable to use, but it may not be ideal for all skin types. While the heartleaf extract and gentle oil formula are intended to cleanse and help remove makeup and blackheads, unfortunately, this product can be a bit too rich for acne-prone or sensitive skin, potentially leading to breakouts.

The cleansing oil is effective at dissolving makeup and impurities, but if you have skin that’s prone to clogging, it might leave a bit of residue that could trigger congestion. It’s disappointing because the scent and texture are pleasant, but the formulation might not suit those who need a non-comedogenic option.

Overall, while the Anua Heartleaf Cleansing Oil is appealing in scent and design, it may not be the best choice for acne-prone skin, as it can cause breakouts for some users.

Name: E.Ellix
Review Title:
2.0 out of 5 stars
 Gave me pimples!
Review Content:
I first started double cleansing with tatcha Camellia oil and then skin 1004 centella cleansing oil (with mini sizes that were sent with other products.) When it came time to buy a new one, I saw that this was a globally loved option — and I LOVE anua heartleaf toner. With those factors, plus the fact that it was the cheapest option, I bought it.

At first I loved the lightweight feel of it, it reminded me of the centella light oil, got all my make up off and made my skin soft before my water cleanse. But the morning after first use I woke up to a big pimple on my chin. I figured it could be a fluke, used it again…I woke up with a new big pimple on my cheek. Still thinking it could be a fluke, even though I NEVER ever ever get pimples like this (if I do, I get tiny rosacea pimples not deep ones like this), I tried once more before buying the Centella oil cleanser.

The day I went back to centella, no more pimples popped up over night.

I am in my 30s, dry, rosacea prone skin.

P.S. If you’re skin is anything like mine, I LOVE the centella light cleansing oil, its worth the extra three dollars or so more that Anua.

Name: BA
Review Title:
2.0 out of 5 stars
 Made My Face Break Out
Review Content:
To start off, I have oily, acne prone skin. I bought this cleansing oil after watching reviews and recommendations online. I found out after making my purchase that some of the ingredients are comedogenic, can clog pores, and break some people out. Despite that, I still wanted to give it a chance.

For what it’s worth, it did work quite well at first. My skin felt a lot smoother and cleaner after including this in my routine. However, about a week in, the clogging effects kicked in, and I started breaking out in cystic acne. These acne spots lasted from 1-2 weeks, and I’m still recovering from some of them.

All in all, it’s probably a good product if your skin likes it, and it just so happened that it wasn’t suited for my skin. I’m currently being cautious about Anua as a brand because their products might not be for me, but I don’t want to judge them just yet. I currently have their Dark Spot Correcting Serum on the way, and I have high hopes for it.

I'm still on the search for a good unscented cleansing oil that will give me the same effect as this one that will rid my pores. If only they would use better ingredients and not use fragrance!

Wished Amazon has a better way of showing honest reviews with 1 or 2 stars. I would not have bought this if I had seen those reviews.

(I should let you know I’ve always been very lucky with my skin and have struggled with little to no acne). The small breakouts progressed to deeper ones I felt below the level of the skin but took FOREVER to come to a head, if they even did. This persisted for months, even when I discontinued the pore cleanser after doing some research on Tik Tok. The pimples left dark marks on my skin after healing as well, some that are still going away now. I’m not sure if this product just did not agree with my particular skin type but my barrier is back to normal after taking this out of my routine!

The cons:

- Oil smells like plastic

I'm also trying the Anua toner pads and that's a miss, too. Looks like Anua and I just weren't meant to be.

Name: Angie
Review Title:
3.0 out of 5 stars
 PERSONAL RECOMMENDATION
Review Content:
Seeing so many good reviews I bought this cleanser to try during a break out, but then I soon learned after more than a month that the cleanser was not beneficial for my dry sensitive skin. Although it broke me out more by texture I was still able to see my natural glow enhance, observing this I strongly recommend for those with dry skin or are sensitive to be cautious ( a 2 week trial or test patch). If you have both like me don't waste your money on this trust, a better choice would be the CENTELLA cleansing oil by SKIN1004, after about 2 weeks it managed to rapidly get rid of the damage the ANUA cleanser caused and with consistency progress to my original skin goal of clear glass skin (also DOESNT contain pore clogging ingredients). I do believe the ANUA cleanser works for most but not all, a reason for that being it DOES contain pore clogging ingredients (a fact I learned from my dermatologist) and is also why I personally recommend to READ THIS REWIEW for more awareness about the product despite the hype.

*Be mindful that the cleansing technique DOES matter, there's a certain way to use oil cleansers.

*After any oil cleanser always use ANOTHER cleanser based on skin type (water, foam, or gel) to get rid of the excess oil in which also preventing possible break outs.

*Both cleansers have a natural SCENT that may be strong for certain people.

I don't know if it's just the normal cycle of my skin or the products are causing more problems. I originally got these products to help with the blackheads, sebaceous filaments, and pores. My pores have looked a little better, but the blackheads and sebaceous filaments have seemed to multiply. I cleanse according to directions but have never actually seen the dirt and oil and all the nasty stuff come out. Maybe I'm still not doing it right??? I think I might switch back to what I originally used to see if it is the new products causing the problems. I'm giving this product 3 stars, because I simply dont know If its working or now.

Name: Victoria
Review Title:
3.0 out of 5 stars
 Irritated my skin - normal to dry (not typically sensitive)
Review Content:
Made my skin red, looked like rosacea (I do not have this condition)...

My skin is not normally sensitive to products and it is very rare that I get red or irritated skin as a result of a product. However, I do use caution when introducing a product into my routine. So, I started with using the product one a week, then twice, gradually increasing the frequency of use. Upon the 5th week, I started noticing redness and irritability in places where I massaged the product in (for pore cleansing, of course). I thought it may be a flaw in my appliance of the cleanser so I reduced frequency, changed the force I used to apply it and the temperature of the water when rinsing, and tried cutting down from 2 cleansers to just this one. I had to pull the plug when I noticed that the redness was worse and lasted longer with each use.

Unfortunately, my trial period took longer than I expected and I missed the return date. I really wanted it to work and was really looking forward to it because the reviews of the product were so great. I recommend trying the product out as soon as you get it to patch test for irritation.

On a positive note, I loved how the product felt when applying; it's super smooth and had a pleasant but not pungent scent. I did notice my pores appearing more clear, but I won't risk the repeated allergic reaction.

Review Title:
3.0 out of 5 stars
 It's a decent cleanser, but caused breakouts
Review Content:
It does a great job breaking up makeup and cleansing the face in general. The smell isn't overwhelming and it's very fluid and watery so it doesn't feel like thick oil on your face. However, it is not for me. I have combination skin and I struggle with closed comedones, so I was hoping this would help clear my clogged pores. It did quite the opposite. Within a week, I had 3 larges pimples right in the center of my forehead as well as other smaller whiteheads forming all throughout my forehead including a random pimple on my cheekbone and another painful one on my chin. this didn't even clear my pores in the slightest. if anything, it doubled my clogged pores.

I know if you are oil cleansing for the first time, purging is to be expected, but this is not my first oil cleansing product which leads me to believe this was clearly a bad reaction to the product. I still think oily/acne prone skin might still benefit from this, just make sure to have a backup plan if things go south. We all have different skin and react to ingredients differently so I don't want to discourage anyone from trying this product. Just be careful and listen to your skin!

Name: Anna
Review Title:
4.0 out of 5 stars
 Cleanser
Review Content:
I recently tried the Anua Heartleaf Pore Control Cleanser, and it has quickly become a staple in my skincare routine. This cleanser has a gentle formula that effectively removes dirt, oil, and makeup without stripping my skin of its natural moisture. The star ingredient, Heartleaf extract, is known for its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties, which makes it perfect for my sensitive skin.

After using this cleanser for a few weeks, I noticed that my pores appear smaller, and my skin feels smoother and less congested. I love that it leaves my skin feeling clean and refreshed without any tightness or dryness. The lightweight gel texture foams up nicely and has a subtle, fresh scent that’s very pleasant.

Overall, I highly recommend the Anua Heartleaf Pore Control Cleanser for anyone looking for a gentle yet effective cleanser that helps to control oil and minimize pores. It’s especially great for those with sensitive or acne-prone skin.

Name: Ela
Review Title:
4.0 out of 5 stars
 Fantastic makeup remover and cleans out pore gunk
Review Content: I am new to oil cleansers but after seeing so much about this product online I decided to give it a try. I usually use this before my regular cleanser at night to remove makeup, sunscreen, etc from the day. This really gets everything off, even eye makeup, and is extremely gentle. The smell is unique but I find it to be really pleasant. It's very thin/fluid, so I did make a bit of a mess before I learned how to angle the pump better to dispense it. I have notices since using this that my skin looks a lot more refined--I have fewer blackheads and enlarged pores, especially around my nose and cheeks. Worth noting also that I tried using this alone as my morning cleanser as others have suggested, but I did not find it sufficient. If you have very dry skin that might work well, but for my combo skin it felt too hydrating and didn't leave my skin feeling 'clean'. This is definitely not a miracle product and unless your skin isn't used to regular cleansing or skincare of any kind, you probably won't see all those blackheads and sebaceous filaments coming out all over your hands as you cleanse like in the viral TikTok ads. It does do a good job though and I definitely recommend giving it a try.

At first the scent threw me off guard, it is not strong however usually skin care products with scents could really make it or break it for me, and in this case it made it. It was really soothing to put onto the skin and really broke down the dirt and makeup from the day for me, after that I used my water based cleanser to rinse off the remainder of the oil cleanser and dirt. I will say this however, make sure that when using this product to get the impact you want you have to massage it onto your face 30-60 seconds that way it can really break down the makeup and dirt that you have on your skin. I use it at least 2-3x week followed with a cleanser and then toner and moisturizer and I have noticed that I have gotten my makeup off in a better way than using micellar water. Really truly recommend this product.

Name: Diana
Review Title:
4.0 out of 5 stars
 Nice for sensitive skin
Review Content:
I've used cleansing oils for years to double cleanse my skin, and typically go back to one or two of my tried and true favorites. Decided to try this one because of the lower price point and its claims to clear blackheads/sebum in pores for glasslike skin; as someone in my 40s with large pores in my T-zone, I'll try anything at least once to see if it helps.

The Good:
+ there's no scent and so far it hasn't irritated my skin while using it
+ there's enough "slip" in the oil that you don't have to pull at your skin to dissolve makeup/sunscreen, but not so slippery that you feel like your face is coated in it
+ emulsifies well with water when washing it off
+ one pump is more than enough product to dissolve even the most waterproof mascara and eyeliner at the end of the day
+ nozzle cover is nice when storing bottle

The Bad:
- the pump isn't very good; it squirts/splashes product too hard (I plan to use up my other cleansing oil & transfer it to that bottle)
- haven't noticed much blackhead/pore reduction, but I don't use it daily

Name: Cairo
Review Title:
5.0 out of 5 stars
 My new fav, possibly one of my holy Grail facial products
Review Content:
This is a good sized amount of product. I originally was going to use two pumps of the product but after the first pump. I could tell I wouldn't need much more so I added a half pump more. So 1.5 pumps total, I could have easily only used .5 or 1 pump. It felt so nourishing on my skin. Like most other double cleansing oils,any times it can leave your vision a little "foggy" for a moment after using, an annoying but harmless side effect. But to my surprise, I didn't have that experience at all. Every product cleansing oil I've tried has always done that to me. So already it was a plus for me.
I'm gently on my eye area so I don't hurt my eyelashes or delicate and thin skin in the eye area..I gently press my fingertips onto the eyelids to "melt" makeup away. I don't ever use a scrubbing motion. This method worked wonderfully as well this time. The cleansing oil spread easily but was not at all runny, nor was it goopy. It was the perfect consistency. No harsh smells or burning or irritating texture against my skin. Great for sensitive and delicate skin. I rinsed my mascara and eyeshadow of easily after 40 seconds of gentle rubbing. No residue left behind and my skin isn't tight and dry and is so wet to the touch. I don't wear much makeup, usually just eyes or long lasting lip color. But I do live in a humid southern USA state and need to get the sticky humid off at the end of a day. Worked wonders. I could have been fine with using just this but I also bought the foam cleanser well and the combination of both was phenomenal for this skincare nerd. I do believe these will last quite some time so makes it great value for the money. Quality skincare and I'm excited to try more of their products!
Pics show my eye makeup before applying, after with it on my skin, and after only rinsing it three times. I used a roll of gift wrapping tape for scale of bottle size.. came with a nice an easy to remove sanitary safety clasp to prevent spillage.

Name: Rhyann Wilson
Review Title:
5.0 out of 5 stars
 Holy grail for stubborn clogged pores!
Review Content: I’m not usually one to write a review of a skincare product because I’ve tried so many and my skin is just so problematic no matter what. This is probably the first time in years that a new product I’ve tried wasn’t a flop. Is it a miracle? No. But my acne is less inflamed, my skin stays hydrated and smooth, and I do think my clogged pores have SLOWLY improved (over the last 2 months). This is really saying a lot coming from me because my clogged pores have not budged for years and this has made a very noticeable difference. My favorite combo is this and a clay mask, it really helps clean out your pores if you struggle with bumps and blackheads. You do have to be consistent to see the benefits but if you can manage to double cleanse at least once a day if not morning and night then I think you’d still see benefits. One important thing to note is that if you get your skin wet before lathering it into your skin then you’ll have to use a lot of product because water thins it out a ton. It’s nice because it rinses off so easily for an oil but you have to remember that you can’t wet your hands or face first (unlike normal cleansers which wouldn’t work like that). Especially if you use it in the shower that can be a bit hard to remember but it’s not a big deal.

Name: tklmepnk
Review Title:
5.0 out of 5 stars
 Give it a try!
Review Content:
This is my first time using Korean beauty products. I had a skincare routine using mostly Cerave and other drugstore products. I knew I needed to find something different when my skin continued to break out and I had no healthy glow. So I decided to take a chance on Anua.

First of all, I've never had a problem with my skin not being soft. My main problems are continuous breakouts on my forehead and chin area as well as redness under my nose and dull looking skin. I start my routine with this pore cleansing oil. I was skeptical at first because...oil? On my skin? But it smelled so good that I decided to give it a shot. I use just one pump and I massage it on my face for a good couple minutes. After using this for a couple days I did notice my skin was softer and starting to look healthier, but I new I was still missing something. So back to Amazon I went and I got the Anua pore cleansing foam.

After using the pore oil, I rinse and follow up with the Anua pore deep cleansing foam, which also smells nice and clean. I lather it up and massage it on my face while paying special attention to my problem areas. After rinsing and patting my face dry, I can already see such a difference with my skin. After a few uses I was getting some glow back and the redness was fading.

I decided to add more Korean products to my skincare routine and since Anua, I have also added BHA exfoliating toner pads and Madagascar centella ampoule. The BHA pads I use directly after the Anua pore cleansing foam. They have a softer side and a rough side for when you need some extra scrubbing. They smell like tea tree oil, which I don't have a problem with but some people do. After using these I pat my face to let the product soak in. Then I follow up with the Madagascar centella ampoule. This stuff is non scented and must be milk from the heavens because oh my goodness. I thought my face was glowing before using this but after? I look like I was kissed by the morning dew. Seriously face has never looked so healthy. It's soft, it's glowing, I'm very pleased.

Review Content:
The Anua Heartleaf Pore Control Cleansing Oil has become a staple in my skincare routine. This Korean skincare gem is not only effective at removing makeup but also gentle on my sensitive skin.

💧 Deep Cleansing Without Stripping
This cleansing oil effectively removes makeup, sunscreen, and even stubborn waterproof products while leaving my skin feeling soft and hydrated. It doesn’t strip away natural oils, which is perfect for maintaining my skin's balance.

✨ Blackhead and Pore Care
I’ve noticed a significant improvement in the appearance of my pores and a reduction in blackheads. The lightweight formula penetrates deep into the pores, lifting impurities and leaving my skin looking clearer and smoother.

🌿 Soothing Heartleaf Extract
Heartleaf is a game-changing ingredient! It soothes irritation and redness, making this oil ideal for those with sensitive or acne-prone skin. My skin feels calm and refreshed after every use.

🌀 Easy to Use
The texture is silky and spreads easily across the skin. It emulsifies well when water is added, making it simple to rinse off without leaving a greasy residue.

🎉 Final Thoughts
The Anua Heartleaf Pore Control Cleansing Oil is an excellent choice for anyone looking for a gentle yet effective cleansing oil. It delivers on its promises of makeup removal and pore care while being soothing and non-irritating. Highly recommend this product for a clean and radiant complexion!

Name: primrosefiberstudio
Review Title:
5.0 out of 5 stars
 Works perfectly well!
Review Content:
I’ve used two oil cleansers before this but was convinced to try this during prime day, after my friend’s recommendation (after her seeing it on TikTok). It’s relatively inexpensive so I thought it was a no-brainer!
I’m pretty pleased with it and will likely repurchase! Because my skin was used to oil cleansers beforehand, this didn’t break me out at all (but my friend claimed she had a purge phase before it calmed down). It lathers well, smells herbal, and only slightlyyy stings if it really gets into your eyes. I also love the pump, the trendy chubbier pumps don’t work nearly as well.
I use two full pumps and massage it into a full face of makeup (mascara included) before hopping in the shower and rinsing it off in there. I follow with cosrx’s good morning cleanser (highly highly recommend, have been using it for YEARS now). After the shower I go in with a BHA toner and first aid beauty ultra repair cream. And my skin has been happy :)

I’m not seeing a huge pore difference but my skin has been relatively break-out free, and really just non-agitated, which is what I strive for. I have oily, acne-prone skin that since has been cured with acutane, but I’ve committed to a no-frills skincare routine that has kept my face perfectly nice :)

Name: Phuong Le
Review Title:
1.0 out of 5 stars
 This does nothing
Review Content:
I genuinely do not understand the hype. Of course, just because I say I don’t like it doesn’t mean it’s worthless. A lot of people love it. It just doesn’t do anything for my skin, personally. I found that it was pretty useless and didn’t do anything advertised.

My skin type: extremely oily, some redness, highly acne prone, non-sensitive naturally but can be sensitized by my strong prescription acne treatments

Texture: A+. Perfect. As someone with very oily skin, I can’t stand anything too thick on my face. This has practically zero viscosity to it. Maybe 5%. It feels just like water and absorbs pretty quickly. 5/5

Smell: This is an unscented product.

Packaging: I don’t like how there’s just a little hole in a flat circle. I feel like the product kind of goes everywhere that way. For a more expensive Kbeauty toner, I wish they invested a little more in the packaging and gave us a really tiny nozzle to help control the amount like on the bigger Round Lab bottles or the new Naturie Hatomugi bottles. 2/5

Hydration: This is just your average hydrating toner. It hydrates, yes. That’s it. It’s very basic and similar to my really cheap $10 Naturie Hatogumi Skin Conditioner for 500ml, and Naturie doesn’t have as many fancy ingredients. However, as long as a toner doesn’t dry out my skin it gets a full score, so 5/5.

Acne: Doesn’t do anything. Not a literal thing. It doesn’t help calm down larger pimples. It doesn’t even get rid of my little milia or white bumps. It doesn’t make anything worse, but it doesn’t make anything better, and for such a hyped product I was so disappointed. I’ve been using this consistently morning and night for at least two months now, and I’ve noticed no difference when I’ve used this. When I go off my prescription acne meds because I went overboard and burnt off the first layer of my skin, this does nothing to ward off acne while I wait for my skin to calm down so I can go back on my meds. 0/5

Soothing: This does nothing to soothe my skin. As mentioned before, I can be stupid and mess up my skin with meds, and this doesn’t soothe my sensitized skin any faster than just washing my face and not putting any further product on my skin does. It has panthenol in it, and in the past panthenol has done my skin well and has soothed me greatly, but the panthenol in this toner does nothing. This toner does nothing in general. 0/5

This feels like just splashing water on my face with like 5% oil content for some extra hydration, but you can do that with any basic, much cheaper toner.

Name: Blueyes
Review Title:
1.0 out of 5 stars
 Probably good for your skin but not for mine!
Review Content: I so badly wanted to like this toner, especially since it has all natural ingredients but my skin wasnt having it. I have severely sensitive skin and break out with a lot of products. I patch tested this behind my ear and the crease of my inner arm where my forearm and bicep meet. No sign of reaction after 48hrs so I started using it on my face. I started noticing more cystic acne appearing to the point where I kept telling myself it's ok I'm just purging and it will get better. It's been a month and no sign of clearing has happened even with my skin cycling. More acne just continued to appear and not go away. I even broke out under my nose which never has happened to me. It didn't sting or burn or itch after applying. It felt super hydrating and was the one product that I didn't think was the culprit but it was the only thing I changed in my routine. I do not have a complex skin routine so it wasn't difficult figuring out that this toner was not agreeing with my skin. I did read that some people can be allergic to heartleaf and this has a high concentration of it. I wouldn't recommend to anyone who breaks out easily but for those whose skin seems to love any product you put on then go for it cause you won't regret it, otherwise, stay clear and do not attempt this toner or else it will wreck havic on your skin like it did mine.

Review Title:

 🤬 FALSE ADVERTISEMENT! 🤬
Review Content:
🤬 I bought this item for sole reason that it was advertised as having a PH of 5.5. Which is extremely important for healthy skin.

I first noticed that the bottle and packaging it's self doesn't have any thing stating a PH of 5.5 so became suspicious.

They weren't expecting me to have a $350 dollar PH meter.
I tested the PH of this product and it is nowhere near 5.5 ph which we all know is what essential for healthy skin.

I have not tested the performance of the product yet, it might be wonderful for all I know. But I do know that skin, especially face skin likes a PH of 5.5 for many reasons including fighting bacteria, moisture and soft, healthy, elastic youthful skin.

Nonetheless, I wouldn't have bought this product if I knew it wasn't PH5.5
False advertisement 🤬

Name: dawndrew
Review Title:
1.0 out of 5 stars
 Worst Breakout EVER
Review Content:
I read reviews and really wanted to love this! I am premenopausal and wanted to replace my Thayers Rose Toner (it was becoming to drying). I have not had a breakout since my hormones have calmed down. What I look for in a toner is picking up any residue or makeup left after double cleansing, balancing my PH and adding some moisture while not compromising my face clean (no sticky residue).
This toner did not burn or irritate my sensitive skin, but I started to get cystic knots under my skin. I read that due to the light about of AHA in this toner, there could initially be some purging. Since my skin was irritated I bought in to this, but every day my face was breaking out in new spots where I HAVE NEVER had acne flare-ups before. I used it for 2 weeks and after two days of quitting this, my face cleared up immediately. I suppose I am allergic to heartleaf or I don't know what, but it is a shame because I gravitate towards simple ingredients and of course the price point of this is not bad for the amount you get. I switched to Cerave hydrating toner and so far, this is working well with the rest of my korean skincare routine......but anything is an improvement from the way this Anua Heartleaf 77 soothing toner wrecked my face!!

Name: Vincent
Review Title:
1.0 out of 5 stars
 It does…nothing.
Review Content: I’m a big fan of Korean skincare, and a skincare enthusiast overall. I consume a lot of skincare content online; although I typically prefer content from cosmetic chemists, and other creators who focus on delivering hard facts, proper skincare education, etc, I did get roped into trying this product when it was overly hyped up in TikTok, mostly because the claims about what it does aligned with what my skin needs. For reference, my skin is typically oily/dehydrated and “reactive” (not sensitive in that I can tolerate a lot of/high potency actives, but some particular Ingredients, such as fragrant ingredients/components wreck my skin barrier) I use a potent retinoid currently to help with oil production, hyperpigmentation, fighting off acne/wrinkles, and it definitely leaves my skin a little drier and more reactive than normal. This toner seemed like it would pair well with the rest of my routine as an additional step to add some hydration, reduce redness and help sooth sensitivity without being overly heavy, but it honestly did absolutely nothing for me at all. I was not impressed upon my initial use, and continued to use the product regularly for two weeks to give it a fair shot. After two weeks of use I came to the conclusion that this is just not an effective product, as it not address any of the concerns it is advertised to, nor was the immediate experience during use pleasant. Literally just feels like splashing water on the skin. I went back to using other toners I was familiar with after discontinuing use and returning this one and saw improvement in my skin within two or three days.

Name: M
Review Title:
2.0 out of 5 stars
 Overhyped, and It's NOT For Sensitive Skin
Review Content:
This is marketed and advertised all over TikTok as something which is meant to be soothing for sensitive skin and will repair your skin barrier. First things first, I noticed a lot of people on TikTok who DON'T have sensitive skin are big fans of this product, so even if you don't believe me, that should be a big tip for you. I'm a pretty sensitive on my face, and for the first time I used it, it was fine.

It's actually great for getting rid of difficult texture like closed comedones on your forehead and stubborn whiteheads on your chin, as well as making you smooth all around. That's because it's an exfoliating toner, they don't advertise it as that, yet again, but it contains ingredients which are slightly exfoliating. I can also assure you that the Heartleaf isn't the only ingredient going to work in the toner and there are definitely other ingredients present.

However, when I used it again it broke me out. It gave me the same kind of acne I get when my skin barrier is enflamed and disrupted. This was only after the 3rd or 2nd use by the way, and I was applying it directly onto my face without a cotton pad.

Big lesson to not just be randomly influenced by products that blow up on TikTok because there's a good chance people are genuinely just trying to get in on the hype as opposed to wanting to actually spread good advice.

If you have oily, NON-sensitive skin, this will definitely work wonders for you. But aside from that, I'd advise to stay away.

Name: Amazon Customer
Review Title:
2.0 out of 5 stars
 kinda pricy and this did absolutely nothing for me
Review Content:
I damaged my skin barrier using too many actives at once and everyone on tiktok and instragram recommended this calming toner. it's even been advertised as Korea's no 1 selling toner. I used this consistently since july of 2023, and have seen no difference in my skin. It definitely did not burn my skin when i had a damaged barrier, but other than that, it did not give me a glow, did not give extra hydration, did not get rid of my acne, did not reduce redness, did not help my rashes, or do anything of the miracle claims that everyone has had from all reviews i've seen...

I opted for a simple routine, used this toner by itself with just a cleanser and the illyoon ceramide moisturizer for about one month. I attribute the moisture to the illyoon cream and not this toner tho, because when i switched to another moisturizer my skin was dry af in patches. I eventually turned to curology to help me with my skin concerns.

for the 3 month's i've used this i would've expected it to do at least one of those things i listed above. It is definitely unscented which is nice, but it gives me the same vibes as... water? The numbuzin No 1 Herb Calming toner has done more for me in 2 weeks than this toner has done for me in 3 months.

Name: Bobby

Muss man selbst wissen ob man damit ggf. die Umwelt belasten möchte.
Kaufen werde ich es nicht erneut, auf einen Toner kann man so oder so getrost verzichten.

text_ko:
Steht alles im Titel. 알코올과 다양한 미세플라스틱? 에른스트하프트?
이는 알코올에 대한 알레르기가 아닙니다. Das Produkt enthält synthetische Polymere, die allerdings vollständig Wasserlöslich sind. 또한 Wasser gelöste Polymere의 Mikroplastik Partikel, aber eben도 마찬가지입니다.
Muss man selbst wissen ob man damit ggf. Umwelt belasten möchte를 죽으십시오.

Name: KL
Review Title:
5.0 out of 5 stars
 Refreshing, lightweight, soothing
Review Content:
For reference, I have dry/combination, sensitive skin that's prone to inflammation and clogged pores.

It took me until almost the end of the bottle for me to make up my mind about this toner. It's been hyped up so much on social media that I ended up caving and trying it, only to end up slightly disappointed when it didn't seem to make much of a difference on my skin. I wasn't expecting miracles or anything but I was hoping it would help reduce the inflammation I have in my cheeks. It did help a bit, but not much so I felt letdown by it. However, after a while I noticed it seemed to help my other products apply more smoothly and absorb into my skin better (kind of like a boosting toner). It's also lightweight, which makes it easy to layer and incorporate into my routine, spreads well, and provides light hydration. It also feels really refreshing when I apply it, so I enjoyed using it. I like that it didn't make my sensitive skin react either. Actually this is the only product I used (other than a ceramide cream) when my skin had a sudden strange reaction to something. It helped soothe my skin and didn't irritate at all. I wouldn't say it actively helped get rid of it faster (it went away on its own eventually), but it didn't exacerbate the problem, and it gave my skin a bit of hydration which was all I was looking for.

Overall, I didn't see any dramatic improvements in my skin, but I think it's a nice product to have especially when my skin is irritated. I might repurchase in the summer (it's cold and dry where I am now) since it's so refreshing, but for now my skin is fine without it. Also, the 150mL bottle lasted me about 9 months with pretty consistent use (I applied it with my hands and layered it twice in the morning).

Name: Jade Jones
Review Title:
5.0 out of 5 stars
 Love it, more than just water!
Review Content: Not sponsored ofc but I want to clear something up! So many people, have the conversation that this is just water. I would have to strongly disagree because you have to use it for what it is attended for. For me, I do have breakouts every so often. I need something to calm it down. If you’re looking to use this for any other reason, even moisture, I would say that it is lacking. However, for a first step toner, I think it pairs very well. I think it’s important to know that not only does is pair very well with other products, it is very lightweight so that it doesn’t cause peeling later on down the line. I personally pair this with an essence directly after putting this on my skin. I don’t have a lot of inflammatory products and if I do have breakouts, using this helps to calm and soothe those big breakouts. Of course, if you use this product, and then pair with something that inflames you later on, you would think it’s not working it can only do so much because you also need to have a good skin care regimen that does not break you out, and that works for your skin. Name: Gabriella Ville
Review Title:
5.0 out of 5 stars
 Anua Heartleaf Toner 1st Impressions
Review Content:
I ordered the Anua Heartleaf toner during the Black Friday sale, and being new to using this brand(and a total novice to skincare regimens…think just wash and go 365), I decided to give it a few days of daily/nightly use to give a well-rounded opinion on the product. Having heard a TON of good things about this brand from big names(SUHO), and different K-Beauty influencers, I definitely had to try it once it became easier to order in the USA.

I have combination skin that gets extremely dry in some areas(worse during the winter), and an obnoxiously oily t-zone year round.
So far, using it the past 2 weeks has calmed down the annoying scaliness I get, and toned down the shine production on my nose. The product is virtually fragrance-free(which is a plus), feels just like water, and the slightly sticky finish when it dries down is really good as a substitute for makeup foundation primer during my routine. At night I use it after washing my face, and put a good lotion/creme on over it, and I wake up feeling SO soft. Of course, do be careful around open cuts or anything because it’ll inevitably sting a little, but not too bad.
Overall, after 2ish weeks of continuous daily use, my skin has felt way softer, smoother, and more uniform in texture than it usually does during the winter months. I’m definitely going to look into adding more Anua products to my routine until my skin is absolutely perfect year-round.
Date: Reviewed in the United States on December 13, 2022

Name: Eva
Review Title:
5.0 out of 5 stars
 Great toner for the price, takes time to work
Review Content: I've been using this toner for a while now and I'm quite pleased with its performance. The barely-there scent is a big plus for me, as I prefer skincare products that don't overwhelm with fragrances. What really stands out is how nourishing it feels on my skin - it's like a refreshing drink for my face. The fact that it's affordable makes it even better, as quality skincare products often come with a hefty price tag. I did notice that it takes a bit of time to show noticeable results, but the patience pays off. The gradual approach seems to suit all skin types, as it doesn't trigger any adverse reactions and can be incorporated into various routines. Overall, if you're looking for a gentle, budget-friendly toner that nurtures the skin over time, this could be a great addition to your skincare regimen.
Date: Reviewed in the United States on August 23, 2023
text_ko: 나는 한동안 이 토너를 사용해 왔고 그 성능에 매우 만족합니다. 저는 향이 과하지 않은 스킨케어 제품을 선호하기 때문에 향이 거의 없는 것이 큰 장점이에요. 정말 눈에 띄는 점은 피부에 영양이 공급되는 느낌입니다. 마치 얼굴에 상쾌한 음료를 마시는 것 같습니다. 고품질 스킨케어 제품에는 종종 높은 가격표가 붙기 때문에 가격이 저렴하다는 사실이 더욱 좋습니다. 눈에 띄는 결과를 보여주기까지는 약간의 시간이 걸린다는 것을 알았습니다.

Name: Rachel R.
Review Title:
5.0 out of 5 stars
 Worth the price
Review Content: So I started upgrading my skincare about a year ago and saw this toner on TT and the girl had GLOWING skin and though a little pricey, her video and my research ght it was worth it. It WAS. Korean girlies got the secrets it seems lol. Within two days the redness from recent period skin and psoriasis was so, soooo much better. A week later and it looks almost perfectly even, even with our 15°F weather! It's gentle, going on like water and drying without any stickiness, had no scent and did not irritate my skin at all. I actually bought this for my bestie's Xmas present, I will never not recommend this product!

text_ko: 그래서 저는 약 1년 전에 스킨케어를 업그레이드하기 시작했고 TT에서 이 토너를 보았는데 그 소녀의 피부는 빛났습니다. 약간 비싸긴 했지만 그녀의 비디오와 제가 조사한 결과는 그만한 가치가 있었습니다. 그랬다. 한국 여자들은 비밀을 갖고 있는 것 같아요 ㅋㅋㅋ. 이틀 만에 최근 생리로 인한 피부 붉어짐과 건선이 정말 많이 좋아졌습니다. 일주일 후 15°F의 날씨에도 불구하고 거의 완벽하게 균일해 보입니다! 물처럼 부드럽게 발리고 끈적임 없이 건조하며 향도 없고 자극도 없어요

text_ko: 내가 사는 모든 제품이 나에게 별로 도움이 되지 않기 때문에 사용하지 않는 것에 쉽게 영향을 받기 때문에 내 욕실 아일랜드를 볼 수 있다면 좋겠습니다. 1박 사용 후 (아침에 사용할 시간도 없었는데) 얼굴이 달라졌어요. 평소 직장에서 자신감 문제로 거울을 잘 안 보는데, 어제 아침에 우연히 거울을 봤는데 뭔가 달라보였어요. 나는 그것이 무엇인지 혼란스러웠습니다. 왜냐하면 제가 한 것은 달리기 때문에 일반 세안제를 사용하는 것뿐이었기 때문입니다.

Name: Veronica A.
Review Title:
5.0 out of 5 stars
 Has become a staple in my routine.
Review Content: I have used this to calm and soothe irritation and redness on my skin. I don't have extremely sensitive skin but I've used it to prep before applying tretinoin and the morning after if needed. It has impressed me with how well it has worked for me. It has become a favorite and part of my daily routine. I will definitely repurchase.
